forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,42 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTCAE5WVKRHTYNJYZT7F2M9K
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8380'
+project: card-comments
+title: Implement comment commands (add/list/get/edit/delete) operating on the inline comments field
+---
+## What
+Create a `comment/` module in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/` providing the operation structs that read and mutate the task's inline `comments` field (a JSON array of `{id, actor, text, timestamp}` member objects). Model the module layout on the existing `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/attachment/` module (mod.rs + add.rs + get.rs + list.rs + update.rs + delete.rs) and the `#[operation(verb, noun)]` macro pattern — BUT the value is stored inline on the task field, NOT as a separate entity (contrast with attachment, which creates its own `attachment` Entity). This honors the "comments are dependent members, not their own entity kind" requirement.
+
+Each comment member is a JSON object: `{ "id": <ulid>, "actor": <actor-id>, "text": <free text>, "timestamp": <ISO 8601 string> }`. The `id` lets get/edit/delete address a specific member. The `timestamp` is set at add time via the same time source the crate already uses (find how `created`/`updated` derive timestamps — see `builtin/definitions/created.yaml` derive-created and the changelog; use chrono/the existing helper for ISO 8601, do not hand-roll).
+
+Files (all under `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/comment/`):
+1. `mod.rs` — declare submodules, re-export the five command structs and a `comment_member_to_json` helper (mirrors `attachment_entity_to_json`).
+2. `add.rs` — `AddComment { task_id, actor, text }` with `#[operation(verb="add", noun="comment")]`. Reads the task via `ctx.entity_context()`, appends a new member (generate ulid id, set timestamp now), writes the task back with `task.set("comments", ...)`. Returns the new member JSON. Validate the actor exists (read `actor` entity; return a clear error if missing).
+3. `list.rs` — `ListComments { task_id }`, `#[operation(verb="list", noun="comments")]`. Returns the comments array (sorted by timestamp ascending — it is a log).
+4. `get.rs` — `GetComment { task_id, id }`, returns one member or `KanbanError::CommentNotFound { id }` (the variant ALREADY EXISTS in error.rs).
+5. `update.rs` — `UpdateComment { task_id, id, text }` edits the `text` of an existing member (who/when are immutable). Errors with CommentNotFound if absent.
+6. `delete.rs` — `DeleteComment { task_id, id }` removes the member by id. Errors with CommentNotFound if absent.
+
+Wire `pub mod comment;` into the crate `lib.rs` (or wherever attachment is declared — check `src/lib.rs`).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `AddComment`, `ListComments`, `GetComment`, `UpdateComment`, `DeleteComment` exist with correct `#[operation]` verb/noun annotations.
+- [ ] A comment member stores who (actor id), what (text), when (ISO 8601 timestamp) and a stable member id; adding preserves existing members.
+- [ ] get/update/delete on a missing id return `KanbanError::CommentNotFound`.
+- [ ] AddComment with a non-existent actor returns a clear actor-not-found error.
+- [ ] Comments are stored inline on the task `comments` field — no `comment` Entity is ever created (assert no comment file appears under the tasks dir).
+- [ ] `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -- -D warnings` clean.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] In `src/comment/add.rs` test module (modeled on `attachment/add.rs` tests): init board, add task + actor, add two comments, re-read task, assert `comments` array has 2 members each with actor/text/timestamp/id; assert the second add preserved the first (`test_add_comment_preserves_existing`).
+- [ ] Test AddComment with unknown actor errors.
+- [ ] Test get/update/delete happy paths and the CommentNotFound error path for a bogus id.
+- [ ] Test that no standalone comment entity file is written (comments live only inline on the task).
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban comment` — green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write add+preserve and CommentNotFound tests first, then implement the structs.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -11,32 +11,50 @@
 ## What
 Create a `comment/` module in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/` providing the operation structs that read and mutate the task's inline `comments` field (a JSON array of `{id, actor, text, timestamp}` member objects). Model the module layout on the existing `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/attachment/` module (mod.rs + add.rs + get.rs + list.rs + update.rs + delete.rs) and the `#[operation(verb, noun)]` macro pattern — BUT the value is stored inline on the task field, NOT as a separate entity (contrast with attachment, which creates its own `attachment` Entity). This honors the "comments are dependent members, not their own entity kind" requirement.

-Each comment member is a JSON object: `{ "id": <ulid>, "actor": <actor-id>, "text": <free text>, "timestamp": <ISO 8601 string> }`. The `id` lets get/edit/delete address a specific member. The `timestamp` is set at add time via the same time source the crate already uses (find how `created`/`updated` derive timestamps — see `builtin/definitions/created.yaml` derive-created and the changelog; use chrono/the existing helper for ISO 8601, do not hand-roll).
+CONFIRMED (research): `write_internal` in `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/context.rs:967` only special-processes `FieldType::Attachment`; a `comment-log` field stores its raw JSON array faithfully and reads back unchanged (no enrichment). So append-and-write works directly on the field value.
+
+Each comment member is a JSON object: `{ "id": <ulid lowercased>, "actor": <actor-id>, "text": <free text>, "timestamp": <ISO 8601 string> }`. The `id` lets get/edit/delete address a specific member. Generate the id with `ulid::Ulid::new().to_string().to_lowercase()` (same as attachment/add.rs). The `timestamp` is an ISO 8601 string set at add time via the crate's existing chrono usage (do not hand-roll formatting).
+
+### Author resolution (who) — single source of truth in `execute()`
+`AddComment.actor` is `Option<String>` (NOT a required field). Resolution happens inside `AddComment::execute()`:
+- `Some(actor)` → validate the actor entity exists (read `actor`); return a clear actor-not-found error if missing.
+- `None` → resolve the default author from **OS-level user identity** and use it; this path NEVER errors for a missing author.
+
+Add a reusable resolver in the **actor domain** (not the comment module): `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/actor/` — e.g. `pub(crate) async fn ensure_os_user_actor(ctx) -> Result<ActorId>`:
+- Resolve OS identity with the `whoami` crate: `whoami::username()` for the id basis (sanitize/slugify to a valid actor id, lowercased) and `whoami::realname()` for the display name (fall back to the username if realname is empty/unknown).
+- Idempotently ensure the actor entity exists via `AddActor::new(id, name).with_ensure()` (CONFIRMED: `actor/add.rs` ensure=true creates-or-returns).
+- Return the actor id.
+- Add `whoami = "1"` to `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/Cargo.toml` (already a workspace dep in `apps/kanban-app/Cargo.toml`; reuse the same major).

-Files (all under `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/comment/`):
+Note: the MCP agent path always dispatches with an explicit actor (`claude-code`), so the OS-user fallback only triggers for actor-less callers (e.g. a human in the desktop app with none configured) — it will never mis-attribute agent comments.
+
+Files (under `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/comment/` unless noted):
 1. `mod.rs` — declare submodules, re-export the five command structs and a `comment_member_to_json` helper (mirrors `attachment_entity_to_json`).
-2. `add.rs` — `AddComment { task_id, actor, text }` with `#[operation(verb="add", noun="comment")]`. Reads the task via `ctx.entity_context()`, appends a new member (generate ulid id, set timestamp now), writes the task back with `task.set("comments", ...)`. Returns the new member JSON. Validate the actor exists (read `actor` entity; return a clear error if missing).
-3. `list.rs` — `ListComments { task_id }`, `#[operation(verb="list", noun="comments")]`. Returns the comments array (sorted by timestamp ascending — it is a log).
-4. `get.rs` — `GetComment { task_id, id }`, returns one member or `KanbanError::CommentNotFound { id }` (the variant ALREADY EXISTS in error.rs).
-5. `update.rs` — `UpdateComment { task_id, id, text }` edits the `text` of an existing member (who/when are immutable). Errors with CommentNotFound if absent.
-6. `delete.rs` — `DeleteComment { task_id, id }` removes the member by id. Errors with CommentNotFound if absent.
-
-Wire `pub mod comment;` into the crate `lib.rs` (or wherever attachment is declared — check `src/lib.rs`).
+2. `add.rs` — `AddComment { task_id, actor: Option<String>, text }` with `#[operation(verb="add", noun="comment")]`. Reads the task via `ctx.entity_context()`, resolves the author (above), appends a new member (ulid id, ISO 8601 timestamp now), writes the task back with `task.set("comments", ...)`. Returns the new member JSON. MUST preserve existing members.
+3. `list.rs` — `ListComments { task_id }`, `#[operation(verb="list", noun="comments")]`. Returns the comments array sorted by timestamp ascending (it is a log).
+4. `get.rs` — `GetComment { task_id, id }`, returns one member or `KanbanError::CommentNotFound { id }` (variant ALREADY EXISTS in error.rs).
+5. `update.rs` — `UpdateComment { task_id, id, text }` edits only the `text` of an existing member (who/when immutable). CommentNotFound if absent.
+6. `delete.rs` — `DeleteComment { task_id, id }` removes the member by id. CommentNotFound if absent.
+7. `actor/` — add `ensure_os_user_actor` resolver (see above) and export it for `add.rs`.
+8. Wire `pub mod comment;` into `src/lib.rs` (next to where `attachment` is declared).

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `AddComment`, `ListComments`, `GetComment`, `UpdateComment`, `DeleteComment` exist with correct `#[operation]` verb/noun annotations.
-- [ ] A comment member stores who (actor id), what (text), when (ISO 8601 timestamp) and a stable member id; adding preserves existing members.
+- [ ] `AddComment` (with `actor: Option<String>`), `ListComments`, `GetComment`, `UpdateComment`, `DeleteComment` exist with correct `#[operation]` verb/noun annotations.
+- [ ] A comment member stores who (actor id), what (text), when (ISO 8601 timestamp) and a stable ulid member id; adding preserves existing members.
+- [ ] `add comment` with an explicit, existing actor attributes that actor.
+- [ ] `add comment` with an explicit, non-existent actor returns a clear actor-not-found error.
+- [ ] `add comment` with `actor: None` resolves the OS user, idempotently ensures that actor entity, attributes it, and succeeds (never errors on missing author).
 - [ ] get/update/delete on a missing id return `KanbanError::CommentNotFound`.
-- [ ] AddComment with a non-existent actor returns a clear actor-not-found error.
 - [ ] Comments are stored inline on the task `comments` field — no `comment` Entity is ever created (assert no comment file appears under the tasks dir).
 - [ ] `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -- -D warnings` clean.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] In `src/comment/add.rs` test module (modeled on `attachment/add.rs` tests): init board, add task + actor, add two comments, re-read task, assert `comments` array has 2 members each with actor/text/timestamp/id; assert the second add preserved the first (`test_add_comment_preserves_existing`).
-- [ ] Test AddComment with unknown actor errors.
-- [ ] Test get/update/delete happy paths and the CommentNotFound error path for a bogus id.
-- [ ] Test that no standalone comment entity file is written (comments live only inline on the task).
+- [ ] `src/comment/add.rs` tests (modeled on `attachment/add.rs`): init board, add task + actor, add two comments with an explicit actor, re-read task, assert `comments` has 2 members each with actor/text/timestamp/id; assert the second add preserved the first (`test_add_comment_preserves_existing`).
+- [ ] Explicit unknown actor errors.
+- [ ] `actor: None` path: no actor passed → asserts a member is written, its `actor` equals the ensured OS-user actor id, and that actor entity now exists (idempotent on repeat).
+- [ ] get/update/delete happy paths + the CommentNotFound error path for a bogus id; update changes text but leaves actor/timestamp unchanged.
+- [ ] Assert no standalone comment entity file is written (comments live only inline on the task).
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban comment` — green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write add+preserve and CommentNotFound tests first, then implement the structs.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write add+preserve, the three author-resolution paths, and CommentNotFound tests first, then implement.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -9,52 +9,49 @@
 title: Implement comment commands (add/list/get/edit/delete) operating on the inline comments field
 ---
 ## What
-Create a `comment/` module in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/` providing the operation structs that read and mutate the task's inline `comments` field (a JSON array of `{id, actor, text, timestamp}` member objects). Model the module layout on the existing `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/attachment/` module (mod.rs + add.rs + get.rs + list.rs + update.rs + delete.rs) and the `#[operation(verb, noun)]` macro pattern — BUT the value is stored inline on the task field, NOT as a separate entity (contrast with attachment, which creates its own `attachment` Entity). This honors the "comments are dependent members, not their own entity kind" requirement.
+Create a `comment/` module in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/` providing (a) the agent-facing operation structs that read and mutate the task's inline `comments` field, and (b) reusable helpers that the UI field-set path (separate task) also uses. The value is stored inline on the task `comments` field as a JSON array of `{id, actor, text, timestamp}` member objects — NOT as a separate entity (contrast with attachment, which creates its own `attachment` Entity). This honors the "comments are dependent members, not their own entity kind" requirement.

 CONFIRMED (research): `write_internal` in `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/context.rs:967` only special-processes `FieldType::Attachment`; a `comment-log` field stores its raw JSON array faithfully and reads back unchanged (no enrichment). So append-and-write works directly on the field value.

-Each comment member is a JSON object: `{ "id": <ulid lowercased>, "actor": <actor-id>, "text": <free text>, "timestamp": <ISO 8601 string> }`. The `id` lets get/edit/delete address a specific member. Generate the id with `ulid::Ulid::new().to_string().to_lowercase()` (same as attachment/add.rs). The `timestamp` is an ISO 8601 string set at add time via the crate's existing chrono usage (do not hand-roll formatting).
+Each comment member is a JSON object: `{ "id": <ulid lowercased>, "actor": <actor-id>, "text": <free text>, "timestamp": <ISO 8601 string> }`. Generate the id with `ulid::Ulid::new().to_string().to_lowercase()`. The `timestamp` is an ISO 8601 string set at add time via the crate's existing chrono usage (do not hand-roll formatting).

-### Author resolution (who) — single source of truth in `execute()`
-`AddComment.actor` is `Option<String>` (NOT a required field). Resolution happens inside `AddComment::execute()`:
-- `Some(actor)` → validate the actor entity exists (read `actor`); return a clear actor-not-found error if missing.
-- `None` → resolve the default author from **OS-level user identity** and use it; this path NEVER errors for a missing author.
+### Reusable helpers (used by BOTH the agent ops here AND the UI field-set normalization task)
+Expose `pub(crate)` helpers from the comment module so the `UpdateEntityField` comment-log branch (separate task) can reuse them — single source of truth for member shape + author rules:
+- `build_comment_member(text, author_id) -> Value` — constructs a new member with fresh ulid id + now() ISO 8601 timestamp.
+- `resolve_comment_author(ctx, explicit: Option<&str>) -> Result<ActorId>` — `Some` → validate the actor entity exists (clear error if missing); `None` → resolve OS-level user identity and ensure that actor exists, never erroring.

-Add a reusable resolver in the **actor domain** (not the comment module): `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/actor/` — e.g. `pub(crate) async fn ensure_os_user_actor(ctx) -> Result<ActorId>`:
-- Resolve OS identity with the `whoami` crate: `whoami::username()` for the id basis (sanitize/slugify to a valid actor id, lowercased) and `whoami::realname()` for the display name (fall back to the username if realname is empty/unknown).
-- Idempotently ensure the actor entity exists via `AddActor::new(id, name).with_ensure()` (CONFIRMED: `actor/add.rs` ensure=true creates-or-returns).
-- Return the actor id.
-- Add `whoami = "1"` to `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/Cargo.toml` (already a workspace dep in `apps/kanban-app/Cargo.toml`; reuse the same major).
+Add the OS-user resolver in the **actor domain** (not the comment module): `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/actor/` — `pub(crate) async fn ensure_os_user_actor(ctx) -> Result<ActorId>`:
+- Resolve OS identity with the `whoami` crate: `whoami::username()` for the id basis (slugify/lowercase to a valid actor id) and `whoami::realname()` for the display name (fall back to username if empty/unknown).
+- Idempotently ensure the actor via `AddActor::new(id, name).with_ensure()` (CONFIRMED: `actor/add.rs` ensure=true creates-or-returns). Return the actor id.
+- Add `whoami = "1"` to `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/Cargo.toml` (already a workspace dep in `apps/kanban-app/Cargo.toml`).

-Note: the MCP agent path always dispatches with an explicit actor (`claude-code`), so the OS-user fallback only triggers for actor-less callers (e.g. a human in the desktop app with none configured) — it will never mis-attribute agent comments.
+### Agent-facing ops (under `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/comment/`)
+Model module layout on `src/attachment/` (mod.rs + add.rs + get.rs + list.rs + update.rs + delete.rs) and the `#[operation(verb, noun)]` macro pattern.
+1. `mod.rs` — declare submodules; re-export the five command structs, `build_comment_member`, `resolve_comment_author`, and a `comment_member_to_json` helper.
+2. `add.rs` — `AddComment { task_id, actor: Option<String>, text }`, `#[operation(verb="add", noun="comment")]`. Reads the task, resolves author via `resolve_comment_author`, appends `build_comment_member(...)`, writes the task back with `task.set("comments", ...)`. Returns the new member JSON. MUST preserve existing members.
+3. `list.rs` — `ListComments { task_id }`, `#[operation(verb="list", noun="comments")]`. Returns the comments array sorted by timestamp ascending.
+4. `get.rs` — `GetComment { task_id, id }` → one member or `KanbanError::CommentNotFound { id }` (variant ALREADY EXISTS in error.rs).
+5. `update.rs` — `UpdateComment { task_id, id, text }` edits only `text` (who/when immutable). CommentNotFound if absent.
+6. `delete.rs` — `DeleteComment { task_id, id }` removes by id. CommentNotFound if absent.
+7. Wire `pub mod comment;` into `src/lib.rs` (next to `attachment`).

-Files (under `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/comment/` unless noted):
-1. `mod.rs` — declare submodules, re-export the five command structs and a `comment_member_to_json` helper (mirrors `attachment_entity_to_json`).
-2. `add.rs` — `AddComment { task_id, actor: Option<String>, text }` with `#[operation(verb="add", noun="comment")]`. Reads the task via `ctx.entity_context()`, resolves the author (above), appends a new member (ulid id, ISO 8601 timestamp now), writes the task back with `task.set("comments", ...)`. Returns the new member JSON. MUST preserve existing members.
-3. `list.rs` — `ListComments { task_id }`, `#[operation(verb="list", noun="comments")]`. Returns the comments array sorted by timestamp ascending (it is a log).
-4. `get.rs` — `GetComment { task_id, id }`, returns one member or `KanbanError::CommentNotFound { id }` (variant ALREADY EXISTS in error.rs).
-5. `update.rs` — `UpdateComment { task_id, id, text }` edits only the `text` of an existing member (who/when immutable). CommentNotFound if absent.
-6. `delete.rs` — `DeleteComment { task_id, id }` removes the member by id. CommentNotFound if absent.
-7. `actor/` — add `ensure_os_user_actor` resolver (see above) and export it for `add.rs`.
-8. Wire `pub mod comment;` into `src/lib.rs` (next to where `attachment` is declared).
+Note: the MCP agent always dispatches with an explicit actor (`claude-code`), so the OS-user fallback only triggers for actor-less callers — it never mis-attributes agent comments.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `AddComment` (with `actor: Option<String>`), `ListComments`, `GetComment`, `UpdateComment`, `DeleteComment` exist with correct `#[operation]` verb/noun annotations.
-- [ ] A comment member stores who (actor id), what (text), when (ISO 8601 timestamp) and a stable ulid member id; adding preserves existing members.
-- [ ] `add comment` with an explicit, existing actor attributes that actor.
-- [ ] `add comment` with an explicit, non-existent actor returns a clear actor-not-found error.
-- [ ] `add comment` with `actor: None` resolves the OS user, idempotently ensures that actor entity, attributes it, and succeeds (never errors on missing author).
-- [ ] get/update/delete on a missing id return `KanbanError::CommentNotFound`.
-- [ ] Comments are stored inline on the task `comments` field — no `comment` Entity is ever created (assert no comment file appears under the tasks dir).
+- [ ] Five ops exist with correct `#[operation]` verb/noun annotations; `AddComment.actor` is `Option<String>`.
+- [ ] `build_comment_member` and `resolve_comment_author` are `pub(crate)` and reusable; `ensure_os_user_actor` lives in the actor module.
+- [ ] A member stores who/what/when + a stable ulid id; adding preserves existing members.
+- [ ] explicit existing actor attributed; explicit non-existent actor errors; `actor: None` resolves+ensures the OS-user actor and succeeds.
+- [ ] get/update/delete on a missing id return `KanbanError::CommentNotFound`; update changes text only.
+- [ ] No `comment` Entity is ever created (assert no comment file under the tasks dir).
 - [ ] `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-kanban -- -D warnings` clean.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `src/comment/add.rs` tests (modeled on `attachment/add.rs`): init board, add task + actor, add two comments with an explicit actor, re-read task, assert `comments` has 2 members each with actor/text/timestamp/id; assert the second add preserved the first (`test_add_comment_preserves_existing`).
-- [ ] Explicit unknown actor errors.
-- [ ] `actor: None` path: no actor passed → asserts a member is written, its `actor` equals the ensured OS-user actor id, and that actor entity now exists (idempotent on repeat).
-- [ ] get/update/delete happy paths + the CommentNotFound error path for a bogus id; update changes text but leaves actor/timestamp unchanged.
-- [ ] Assert no standalone comment entity file is written (comments live only inline on the task).
+- [ ] `add.rs` tests (modeled on `attachment/add.rs`): add two comments (explicit actor), re-read, assert 2 members w/ actor/text/timestamp/id and that the second add preserved the first.
+- [ ] Explicit unknown actor errors; `actor: None` path writes a member whose `actor` is the ensured OS-user id and that actor entity now exists (idempotent on repeat).
+- [ ] get/update/delete happy paths + CommentNotFound for a bogus id; update leaves actor/timestamp unchanged.
+- [ ] Assert no standalone comment entity file is written.
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban comment` — green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write add+preserve, the three author-resolution paths, and CommentNotFound tests first, then implement.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write add+preserve, author-resolution paths, and CommentNotFound tests first, then implement.
\ No newline at end of file

The `timestamp` is set at add time as **UTC RFC3339** via the crate's existing chrono usage (`chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339()` — do not hand-roll formatting, and do NOT use local-offset timestamps: boards sync across machines via git and mixed-offset ISO strings don't sort lexically).
+
+ORDERING: the canonical order of a comment log is member `id` ascending — ULIDs are time-ordered, so id order == creation order, and ids are unique (no same-millisecond timestamp ties). Edits preserve `id`, so order is stable under edit.

@@ -13,13 +13,11 @@

 CONFIRMED (research): `write_internal` in `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/context.rs:967` only special-processes `FieldType::Attachment`; a `comment-log` field stores its raw JSON array faithfully and reads back unchanged (no enrichment). So append-and-write works directly on the field value.

-Each comment member is a JSON object: `{ "id": <ulid lowercased>, "actor": <actor-id>, "text": <free text>, "timestamp": <UTC RFC3339 string> }`. Generate the id with `ulid::Ulid::new().to_string().to_lowercase()`. The `timestamp` is set at add time as **UTC RFC3339** via the crate's existing chrono usage (`chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339()` — do not hand-roll formatting, and do NOT use local-offset timestamps: boards sync across machines via git and mixed-offset ISO strings don't sort lexically).
-
-ORDERING: the canonical order of a comment log is member `id` ascending — ULIDs are time-ordered, so id order == creation order, and ids are unique (no same-millisecond timestamp ties). Edits preserve `id`, so order is stable under edit.
+Each comment member is a JSON object: `{ "id": <ulid lowercased>, "actor": <actor-id>, "text": <free text>, "timestamp": <ISO 8601 string> }`. Generate the id with `ulid::Ulid::new().to_string().to_lowercase()`. The `timestamp` is an ISO 8601 string set at add time via the crate's existing chrono usage (do not hand-roll formatting).

 ### Reusable helpers (used by BOTH the agent ops here AND the UI field-set normalization task)
 Expose `pub(crate)` helpers from the comment module so the `UpdateEntityField` comment-log branch (separate task) can reuse them — single source of truth for member shape + author rules:
-- `build_comment_member(text, author_id) -> Value` — constructs a new member with fresh ulid id + now() UTC RFC3339 timestamp.
+- `build_comment_member(text, author_id) -> Value` — constructs a new member with fresh ulid id + now() ISO 8601 timestamp.
 - `resolve_comment_author(ctx, explicit: Option<&str>) -> Result<ActorId>` — `Some` → validate the actor entity exists (clear error if missing); `None` → resolve OS-level user identity and ensure that actor exists, never erroring.

 Add the OS-user resolver in the **actor domain** (not the comment module): `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/actor/` — `pub(crate) async fn ensure_os_user_actor(ctx) -> Result<ActorId>`:
@@ -31,10 +29,10 @@

 1. `mod.rs` — declare submodules; re-export the five command structs, `build_comment_member`, `resolve_comment_author`, and a `comment_member_to_json` helper.
 2. `add.rs` — `AddComment { task_id, actor: Option<String>, text }`, `#[operation(verb="add", noun="comment")]`. Reads the task, resolves author via `resolve_comment_author`, appends `build_comment_member(...)`, writes the task back with `task.set("comments", ...)`. Returns the new member JSON. MUST preserve existing members.

 5. `update.rs` — `UpdateComment { task_id, id, text }` edits only `text` (who/when immutable). CommentNotFound if absent.

 7. Wire `pub mod comment;` into `src/lib.rs` (next to `attachment`).

 Note: the MCP agent always dispatches with an explicit actor (`claude-code`), so the OS-user fallback only triggers for actor-less callers — it never mis-attributes agent comments.
